Elsewhere, Delko loses his badge and sets out to find it before it falls into the wrong hands(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine's teeth and fingertips are found in a pickpocket's stolen purse, and Ducky discovers he had been tortured on a rack before dying. The agents realise he may have been killed in error, and that the real target was his ex-girlfriend, a skilled player of computer games who unwittingly uncovered a mysterious code. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly and Sean Murray(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A millionaire former CIA man meets a premature end when someone plants a controlled explosive in his lighter. Over the years he had built up an impressive list of enemies, so finding his killer looks like an uphill task, but as the agents start working through the potential culprits, a recovered computer file leads them to an escort service used by a wanted hitman. However, what they don't bank on is the chief suspect turning up dead(n)

programme.desc: The new practice manager starts work amid much confusion. Jimmi searches for a new car and Ruhma deals with a woman whose superstitions are troubling her in the build-up to a birth(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The Roman Catholic priest spies trouble when Lady Felicia returns to Kembleford from Africa, having split from her lover Benedict Northam. Mark Williams stars(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley heads on a house hunting mission in rural Devon for a couple who are looking for their first home together, with a budget of £525,000(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore turns to Neil Wragg to come up with a creative new use for an old kitesurfing sail, while the Bristol Upholstery Collective is tasked with turning a forlorn futon into something fabulous. Sarah also takes on the ambitious project of transforming a wine bottle into lighting(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The restorers get to work on a satchel owned by a prisoner of war, a pub clock with an intriguing past and a sea-serpent pen holder. Presented by Jay Blades(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat, hosted by Matt Baker and Angellica Bell(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ben's conscience kicks in when he realises how much Martin hates working for him, Gray tells Chantelle he is scared she will leave him and Max is humiliated when a customer at the club assumes he is Ruby's dad. Karen railroads Billy into giving her a job at the E20 and Jack steps in when a situation gets out of hand in a courtroom, oblivious to the identity of the man he has arrested(n)

programme.title: Inside the Supermarket
programme.desc: There is excitement in the air as the Sainsbury's staff prepare for a party to celebrate the supermarket's 150th year of trading, especially as the Queen will be there. After 50 years' service on the tills, Jenny from the Wallington store has been selected to meet Her Royal Highness and she cannot wait. As the supermarket celebrates its past, it also looks to the future by trialling an experiment to remove all the checkouts from one store, encouraging customers to pay with their mobiles. Will it change the face of shopping or simply send customers elsewhere?(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Actor Danny Dyer wants to know more about his tough working-class family in the East End of London and hopes his history will 'freak a few people out'. But he gets more than he bargained for when he unearths an extraordinary lineage stretching back to the 11th century(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Bolton, Greater Manchester, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: A mix of light-hearted gossip and serious analysis about the process to leave the EU - and no doubt the forthcoming General Election - with Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason(n)

programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood lines up with some serious firepower, working as a US marshal in San Antonio, Texas. His mentor is Deputy Chris Bozeman, who's spent the last 15 years arresting dangerous and wanted men. After rigorous firearms and tactical training, Dom is briefed on new intel and joins live cases, executing arrest warrants on armed suspects around the city(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The return of the programme in which experts try to restore family heirlooms and treasured possessions to their former glories. In the first edition, Jay Blades and the team take on an ornate walnut piano stool, a speedometer from a 1920s vintage car and baby's wicker crib(n)

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores New South Wales by train, travelling 570 miles from Sydney to the mining city of Broken Hill. He explores the origins of Sydney as a penal colony, visiting the Hyde Park barracks where many of the first convicts were housed. He also tours the city's iconic opera house, and volunteers for the lifesaving club at Bondi Beach. In the Blue Mountains, he learns about people's relationship to the land from his indigenous guide, before reaching Katoomba, where he rides the steepest railway in the world(n)

programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal chats to Saffron Barker and AJ Pritchard, who avoided elimination on Sunday night, and previews the songs the couples will be dancing to next week(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: John Cooper Clarke and Phill Jupitus embark on the cross-country treasure-hunting challenge in East Anglia, as well as taking a look at the Norwich School of 19th-century artists(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The first heat of the week sees three chefs challenged to prepare a croque monsieur in 15 minutes for Marcus Wareing, while Monica Galetti sets three more the task of making pea risotto. The six contestants move on to the Signature Dish stage, where they must inspire both judges with their own unique creation before three are chosen to go through to the quarter final and three leave the contest(n)

programme.desc: The chef explores Burgundy and the Auvergne, sampling a sorrel soup made by a chateau owner from the produce of the garden and showcasing Salers beef and Cantal cheese. Back in his Provencal country kitchen, Rick prepares Cantal Bourriols - buckwheat pancakes made with farmhouse eggs and Cep mushrooms(n)

programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present action from the 11th round of fixtures, including Los Angeles Rams v Chicago Bears(n)

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Warship
programme.desc: Aircraft carrier HMS Queen Elizabeth sets out on its first transatlantic crossing, a four-month deployment on the high seas which has the primary purpose of putting the top secret F-35B Lightning Stealth Fighter to the test. Within hours of leaving Portsmouth, the vessel encounters a Russian warship and needs to take immediate evasive action, and the crew has to deal with floods and dangerous fuel leaks on their way to Florida(n)

programme.title: Hindus: Do We Have a Caste Problem?
programme.desc: Parle Patel investigates the hereditary class system of Hindu society, asking what kind of impact does it have on other Hindus living in Britain. He meets a young woman who is so proud of her caste she is still working in the same occupation as her ancestors did thousands of years ago, and also speaks to a man who has rejected his religion because he believes it's the only way to escape his caste(n)

programme.title: Flatmates
programme.desc: Yas thinks that Lauren needs to have some fun after clearing thing sup with Darren, and pushes her into giving online dating a try. After a series of far from romantic encounters, she decides she has had enough, but Yas is adamant she give it one last try and arranges a double date for them both - but Yas is surprised to learn that her companion for the evening is female(n)

programme.title: Flatmates
programme.desc: Craig tries to convince his friends to hold a flat-warming party - but fails to keep it a secret from the landlady(n)

programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood gets a taste of life as a police officer in Aransas Pass on the Texas Gulf Coast, an area with a crime rate 90 per cent higher than the rest of the state(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop

Couple Rita and Pete have both been diagnosed with Type 2 Diabetes, and Gregg and Chris are stunned by this couple's serious sugar addiction when they oversee their shopping(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: British-born actress Jane Seymour knows there is Jewish ancestry on her father's side of the family, but beyond that she knows very little, other than her own suspicions that they suffered in the Holocaust. As she researches her paternal great-aunts Jadwiga and Michaela, she soon finds out more about their experiences under Nazi occupation. The search takes her to Warsaw and France, uncovering stories of life in a ghetto and a terrifying flight to Switzerland from the Germans(n)

programme.desc: Culinary challenge hosted by Sheree Murphy. This time, top chef Mary Ann Gilchrist puts four home cooks through a series of challenges, including how to make a sauce Maltaise(n)

programme.desc: Peter Andre and Joe Pasquale travel around Kent in search of antiques, assisted by experts Paul Laidlaw and Catherine Southon. Joe persuades expert Catherine Southon to buy a pommel horse, while Paul Laidlaw impresses his celebrity sidekick Peter with a pressed poppy from the battlefields of the First World War(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The second heat of the week sees three chefs challenged to cook monkfish with vermouth sauce for Marcus, while three more chefs make a crepe souffle with a caramel sauce for Monica. For the second challenge, the contestants have to prepare a dish of their own design to impress the judges and earn their place in the quarter final(n)

programme.title: Guilt
programme.desc: Max sits in on Angie's police interview and the truth about Walter's death is getting closer to being revealed. To protect his brother, the lawyer orders Jake to leave the country with Angie, telling him he will sort everything out. But Angie is still not convinced they can trust Max. Having uncovered Sheila's guilty secret, Max thinks he finally has the upper hand over his blackmailer, and with the tables finally turned, Max offers Sheila a tempting deal to bring matters to a close - once and for all(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice: You're Fired
programme.desc: The companion programme to The Apprentice, with Tom Allen talking to the eighth candidate to be ejected from the competition. The host is also joined by a panel of experts and fans, who offer an entertaining analysis of this week's task(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Million Dollar Wedding Planner
programme.desc: The exclusive world of Lelian Chew, who organises nuptials for the super-rich in the Far East, taking on 10 just ceremonies a year at an average cost of $1million each. Bespoke Cartier jewellery, hundreds of thousands of dollars of flowers and multiple designer wedding dresses are nothing out of the ordinary. But Lelian's job also involves navigating the clash between age-old Chinese marriage customs and the desires of the young, often with outlandish solutions - multiple weddings are becoming the norm for the Chinese super-rich(n)

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels across Central America, from the Caribbean to the Pacific. Along the way, he goes diving on a coral reef on the coast of Belize, and witnesses a gun battle between park rangers and intruders in Chiquibul National Park. He also discovers how climate change has left Guatemala devastated by famine and drought, reports on measures to curb gang violence in El Salvador, and learns how Costa Rica aims to become the world's first carbon neutral country by 2021(n)

programme.title: Inside the Vatican
programme.desc: The second of two programmes in which cameras record the inner workings of the Vatican over the course of a year. One of the most important tools of reform the Pope possesses is his power to appoint new cardinals. It's the closest he gets to succession planning, because the cardinals will elect the new Pope at the next conclave. Here, Pope Francis selects 14 new cardinals, breaking with tradition by choosing men outside the centres of power. As the pontiff prepares to make a first papal visit to Ireland, the Church is rocked by a report alleging the cover-up of sex abuses by priests, dating back decades(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: A Yorkshire shepherd and his business partner hope the Dragons will flock to invest in their field-to-fork lamb delivery boxes. An inclusive fashion designer brings a little street style to the Den and a confectionery pioneer hopes to bag a sweet Dragon deal with his low sugar, dairy-free chocolate(n)

programme.title: Flatmates
programme.desc: Lauren's big break at work coincides with her time of the month, but she is unstoppable(n)

programme.title: Flatmates
programme.desc: A snow storm traps the group indoors and they struggle to find things to do to keep them occupied(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood samples life working as a game warden in Cameron County, Texas, where he joins warden Mike Gonzalez to learn about the challenges he faces in this role(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Three treasured family heirlooms are brought back to life, including a rare bicycle side car, a Victorian sewing stand and military drum(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team travel to Plymouth to meet the Morfey family, who sold their multi-level home and bought an old wrought-iron forge with the intention of renovating it for their two eldest sons, who have cerebral palsy. But when their daughter got leukaemia and moved miles away for treatment, supported by mum Tara, the family was torn apart. Now the house remains half-built, with no kitchen, hot water or living space. What the Morfeys need is a little help to bring the family back together(n)

programme.title: Coast and Country Auctions
programme.desc: A visit to the Bala Autumn Sale in Snowdonia, north Wales, where veteran seller Emrys Jones is hoping to get well over £2,000 for each of his dogs - Jim and Ross - at auction(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Actor and stage director Derek Jacobi enjoyed a relatively humble upbringing in Leytonstone, London, where his father ran a sweet shop and his mother worked in a drapery, but research into his maternal side of the family soon uncovers a link to a far more intriguing past. Discovering that his great-grandmother's name was Salome Laplain, Jacobi finds that he is descended from a wealthy French Huguenot who fled religious persecution, and who has connections to royalty on this side of the channel(n)

programme.title: Yes Chef
programme.desc: Matt Gillan puts three cooks through a series of culinary challenges, including how to make poached eggs with hazelnut emulsion. Only one of the amateurs can join him in the final(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.desc: Anita Manning and Mark Stacey search through the items brought to the RAF Museum in Hendon, London, and Paul Martin visits an exhibition of First World War flying pioneers(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: YolanDa Brown, the Rev Kate Bottley, Joel Dommett and John Thomson test their knowledge in a series of games across the week, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball dishes the backstage gossip, talks to some of the couples and has a sneak peek at a few of Saturday's costumes with designer Vicky Gill(n)

programme.desc: Musician pals Emeli Sande and Naughty Boy seek out antiques with the help of auctioneers Catherine Southon and Charlie Ross. Alon the way they discover the story of the Scottish connection at Kew Gardens and pull out all the stops on a mighty Wurlitzer cinema organ. But who will be victorious at the auction?(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The six strongest chefs from the heats compete in the quarter-final, inventing a dish that uses coffee to its full potential. After the weakest two are sent home, the others cook two courses for three critics - Jay Rayner, Tom Parker Bowles and Grace Dent. However, judges Monica Galetti and Marcus Wareing will ultimately decide who deserves a place in Knockout Week(n)

programme.title: Giri/Haji
programme.desc: As the emotional impact of Hotaka's death takes its toll, the Moris set aside their conflict and hold a makeshift vigil where they make Yuto an offer - but it will mean betraying his brother. Rodney continues to deny his feelings of guilt over Tiff's death, while in Tokyo, Rei and Natsuko break Sora and Eiko out of the safe house and hit the road, with a pair of yakuza hot on their trail. Kenzo and Yuto's plans to return to Tokyo are derailed by Ian, who threatens to pass photos of them at Sarah's flat to the police(n)

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Noel Gallagher's High Flying Birds return with music from two of the EPs they have released this year, Harry Styles makes his debut with his new single Lights Up and Tom Jones performs a Jerry Lee Lewis number, ahead of his co-hosting role on tomorrow night's show. Alabama Shakes singer and guitarist Brittany Howard has music from her debut solo LP, Jamie, musician and visual artist FKA Twigs performs and the line-up is completed by South African pianist Abdullah Ibrahim(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett(n)

programme.title: Reggie in China
programme.desc: Reggie Yates travels to China and visits four very different cities. His first stop is Shenzhen, which over the past 40 years has grown from a region dominated by fishing and farmland to a bustling metropolis that is home to 12 million people and at the centre of the country's technological revolution. He meets the inventors of an earpiece that uses algorithms to translate speech into other languages, discovers more about the differing attitudes between generations, and witnesses a demonstration of a flying motorcycle(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming explores the English canal network, beginning with a journey from Shardlow to Etruria Junction, Stoke on Trent, exploring the Trent and Mersey Canal. However, not everything goes to plan, and he will have to deal with a tricky issue with his propeller as well as navigating the complicated locks on the Trent and Mersey Canal(n)

programme.title: Princess Margaret: The Rebel Royal
programme.desc: Part two of two. Experts analyse the Princess's marriage to Lord Snowdon at the start of the 1960s. At home with celebrities and artists, they're riding the wave of a cultural and sexual revolution that's transforming Britain. The mystique of monarchy is being increasingly undermined by the emergence of a more open and egalitarian society(n)

programme.title: Greg Davies: Looking for Kes
programme.desc: The comedian pays tribute to Ken Loach's 1969 adaptation of Barry Hines' classic novel A Kestrel for a Knave. He travels to Barnsley to meet members of Hines' family, including his brother Richard, who was the inspiration for the book's protagonist Billy Casper. He also talks to Loach and Dai Bradley, who played Billy in the film, and talks to Jarvis Cocker about the character's impact on British pop culture(n)

programme.title: Kes: Reimagined
programme.desc: Dance and puppetry performance based on Barry Hines' novel A Kestrel for a Knave and Ken Loach's acclaimed 1969 film adaptation. The piece tells the story of a boy struggling with a troubled home and school life in a Yorkshire mining town, but who finds respite from his bleak existence in training a kestrel(n)

programme.title: Sleuths, Spies & Sorcerers: Andrew Marr's Paperback Heroes
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores the conventions of the fantasy genre, profiling key figures including JRR Tolkien, CS Lewis and Terry Pratchett and chatting to best-selling writers Neil Gaiman, Alan Garner and Frances Hardinge(n)

programme.title: Make! Craft Britain
programme.desc: The novice crafters discover the ancient art of silver jewellery making, and origami expert Sam Tsang demonstrates how to make a snack box(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Thomas Shelby's long-awaited day has arrived, but in the middle of the celebrations, a mysterious visitor puts the entire Shelby family in danger, pulling the mob boss into a web of intrigue more lethal than anything he has yet encountered. Period crime drama, starring Cillian Murphy(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: On the orders of the secretive Economic League, Tommy goes to London to meet the wildly eccentric and volatile Duke Leon - and when he is given his mission, he soon realises the extreme lengths his new paymasters are willing to go to in their quest for power. Back in Birmingham, John's temper gets the better of him, and a personal dispute with the Italians threatens to explode into a devastating war. Tommy struggles to regain control of the situation - only to discover how extensive his new enemy's hold on the Peaky Blinders is. Cillian Murphy, Helen McCrory and Joe Cole star, with an appearance by Paddy Considine(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming explores the English canal network. A low canal tunnel in Staffordshire proves to be problematic, and Robbie helps out a stranded boater(n)

programme.title: Sacred Wonders of Britain
programme.desc: Neil Oliver investigates the hallowed sites of ancient Britain, looking at how people expressed their religious beliefs by reshaping the landscape around them. He begins by examining the very first stirrings of religion, visiting Nottinghamshire to view clues to a world of magic and ritual etched into the rock of Creswell Crags by Ice Age hunters, while tombs In the south of England and in the Scottish Borders are evidence of ancestor worship among the farmers of the Neolithic age(n)

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits a secret location in the Cotswolds, which appears to be a high-status Anglo Saxon cemetery, where she finds a very precious and fragile artefact(n)

programme.title: Hunt v Lauda: F1's Greatest Racing Rivals
programme.desc: Documentary examining the battle between Britain's James Hunt and Austria's Niki Lauda for the 1976 Formula 1 world drivers' championship, which was decided by the last grand prix of the season. Featuring rare archive footage and interviews with team managers, families, journalists and friends(n)

programme.title: Sir Chris Hoy: 200mph at Le Mans
programme.desc: Documentary following the Olympic cyclist as he retrains as a racing driver to pursue a lifelong dream of competing in the world's toughest endurance race, the Le Mans 24 Hours. His new sporting career is nearly brought to an abrupt end when the team he has joined is forced to withdraw from the competition. He is able to rejoin the event with a new team, that has never entered Le Mans before, but torrential rain and technical troubles bring home the danger of endurance racing(n)

programme.title: Everyday Miracles: The Genius of Sofas, Stockings and Scanners
programme.desc: Part one of two. Professor Mark Miodownik provides an insight into the design techniques that contributed to the invention and production of everyday objects often taken for granted. He begins by focusing on items found in the home, including razor blades, tights, plywood and foam rubber(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Tommy responds to the Italians' actions, setting him on a path of deadly vengeance that threatens to take him to his darkest place yet - and could also splinter the family. And when he meets Grand Duchess Izabella, the real force behind his mission, he discovers there's a traitor in his paymasters' midst - one who could unravel the entire plan. Meanwhile, Polly realises the depths of her new friend's intentions for her, and Linda implores an increasingly guilty Arthur to give up the criminal life(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Tommy shares some important family news with his brothers, and outlines his plans for the most audacious criminal act the gang has ever undertaken. He also finds himself playing a dangerous game with Tatiana as they attempt to acquire information from each another. Meanwhile, a guilty Polly makes an ill-advised confession - with terrible consequences for her nephew. Cillian Murphy stars, with Helen McCrory, Paddy Considine and Gaite Jansen(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: The Russians test the Shelbys and their gang, and Tommy begins to realise he may have underestimated his new business partners, and has now been seriously outmanoeuvred. As aristocrats exiled by the Soviet Revolution, the Petrovnas have absolutely nothing to lose, and beneath their civilised veneer lies a fierce determination to regain what they have lost. However, the Birmingham mobster still has an ace up his sleeve, in the form of an old enemy now working as an ally. The question is, can he control him? Tom Hardy returns as menacing mobster Alfie Solomons, with Cillian Murphy, Helen McCrory, Annabelle Wallis and Paul Anderson(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming's exploration of the English canal network continues across the Pennines from Marple to Huddersfield, where his journey is hampered by a fallen tree and a leaking lock pound(n)

programme.title: Wonders of the Solar System
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox examines the physics that explains how the solar system was formed from a chaotic cloud of gas. He compares its shaping with a tornado sweeping through Oklahoma, investigates Saturn's rings and demonstrates the influence of that planet's 60 moons - including Enceladus, with its spears of ice that are shot kilometres into space(n)

programme.title: Bob Monkhouse: The Last Stand
programme.desc: Cameras capture the quiz show host and comedian in the summer of 2003 - just months before his death - as he entertains a room full of fellow comics with stand-up, chat and a masterclass in his craft. The night became the stuff of legend among comedians, but was never transmitted in full until recently(n)

programme.title: Talking Comedy
programme.desc: Angela Thorne narrates a look back at clips of the comedian and presenter's appearances on some of the BBC's best-loved talk shows, which have become classic viewing(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of Sue Townsend (Aged 68 3/4)
programme.desc: Julie Walters narrates a profile of the author and creator of Adrian Mole. Sue Townsend left school at 14 with no qualifications, and was struggling to support three children when her writing career began. The critical and commercial success of the first Adrian Mole book led to her becoming one of the best-selling writers of the 1980s. Featuring contributions from friends, family, comedians and writers she inspired, and pupils at her former school, as well as entries from her own diary, documenting her struggles with ill health(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: The third run of Steven Knight's period drama reaches its final episode, and yet again Cillian Murphy as Thomas Shelby finds himself in a seemingly impossible situation. Has his criminal life finally caught up with the Birmingham-based mobster? Tommy prepares to commit the most audacious crime of his career, only to receive an unexpected blow that puts him under unbearable pressure. In a nerve-shredding race against time, Tommy is forced to confront hard truths about himself and his family that could change the Peaky Blinders for ever. As he faces his worst fears he needs help more than ever - but is increasingly uncertain about who he can trust(n)

A troubled Elliot tries to rally support for Jac ahead of Darwin's Mortality and Morbidity review, and Xavier's conscience is pricked when an old acquaintance is admitted, and begins to reconsider his working relationship with Donna(n)

programme.title: Gold Digger
programme.desc: Julia brings Benjamin to her Devon home, and he is taken aback to see the extent of her wealth. The visit is disrupted by a tense confrontation with Ted, who questions Julia's motivations for forming a new relationship. Julia opts to return to London, and asks to meet Benjamin's friends - but when he refuses to show her where he lives, she begins to fear he is hiding something from her. Della turns up unannounced on her ex's doorstep, and is put under pressure by her brother into digging up dirt on Benjamin(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: This time, Nick Knowles and the team transforms a home in Bromsgrove. Karis, a 28-year-old woman who has cerebral palsy, currently lives with her mother, Karen, but now her own health is deteriorating and it is making caring for Karis almost impossible. The family have decided that Karis should move in with her older sister Jo, but her entire home is inaccessible for Karis, so the team decides to build a large two-storey extension to make the house more suitable. programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: In the eighth week of the competition, the candidates are challenged to host a corporate away day onboard the iconic Belmond British Pullman Train, arranging food, drinks and entertainment and also securing a good ticket price. programme.title: Inside the Supermarket
programme.desc: There is excitement in the air as the Sainsbury's staff prepare for a party to celebrate the supermarket's 150th year of trading, especially as the Queen will be there. After 50 years' service on the tills, Jenny from the Wallington store has been selected to meet Her Royal Highness and she cannot wait. As the supermarket celebrates its past, it also looks to the future by trialling an experiment to remove all the checkouts from one store, encouraging customers to pay with their mobiles. Will it change the face of shopping or simply send customers elsewhere?(n)

programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood samples life working as a game warden in Cameron County, Texas, where he joins warden Mike Gonzalez to learn about the challenges he faces in this role(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits a flat in Newcastle with a mixture of styles, and also heads to Bradford to see a three-bed semi. Martin is in Porth, south Wales to view a terraced property(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Undercover filming exposes one man's attempt to cheat his way to a £1million pay out, and a prisoner finds himself with additional time behind bars after a failed scam(n)

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Merseyside police hunt down ill-gotten gains in an affluent home and how one of Britain's most successful fraudsters has a treasured possession seized(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Gareth lends Jimmi a helping hand, and Valerie finds herself caught up in a very awkward situation. Meanwhile, Daniel has another session with Silas(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Mrs McCarthy's friend is the latest villager to die in her sleep, and Father Brown soon senses something sinister is afoot. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton meet a couple seeking a home in rural Shropshire for their £500,000 budget. He also discusses ethical pig farming with mother Sam Gray(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades rescues a set of glass lampshades, an oak sideboard and a pair of printers' trays heading for the tip and invites a couple of friends to bring them back to life(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Three treasured family heirlooms are brought back to life, including a rare bicycle side car, a Victorian sewing stand and military drum(n)

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: By Aontú(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: This time, Nick Knowles and the team transforms a home in Bromsgrove. Karis, a 28-year-old woman who has cerebral palsy, currently lives with her mother, Karen, but now her own health is deteriorating and it is making caring for Karis almost impossible. The family have decided that Karis should move in with her older sister Jo, but her entire home is inaccessible for Karis, so the team decides to build a large two-storey extension to make the house more suitable. Elsewhere, Billy takes on jousting at Warwick Castle(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: In the eighth week of the competition, the candidates are challenged to host a corporate away day onboard the iconic Belmond British Pullman Train, arranging food, drinks and entertainment and also securing a good ticket price. The task will require the teams to show off their organisation and hospitality skills as they hope to impress the corporate clients. For one team the journey gets off to a bumpy start, while the others are left red faced when they have to deal with hungry guests. Back in the boardroom, one of the candidates is sent home from the competition(n)

programme.title: Ask the Leaders
programme.desc: Victoria Derbyshire presents debate between the leaders of the main political parties. First shown on BBC One on Wednesday 20 November(n)

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the final episode of the series, Carol and Aimee, a mother and daughter from Bristol, face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains

Sarah also takes on the ambitious project of transforming a wine bottle into lighting(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ben's conscience kicks in when he realises how much Martin hates working for him, Gray tells Chantelle he is scared she will leave him and Max is humiliated when a customer at the club assumes he is Ruby's dad. Karen railroads Billy into giving her a job at the E20 and Jack steps in when a situation gets out of hand in a courtroom, oblivious to the identity of the man he has arrested(n)

programme.title: Inside the Supermarket
programme.desc: There is excitement in the air as the Sainsbury's staff prepare for a party to celebrate the supermarket's 150th year of trading, especially as the Queen will be there. After 50 years' service on the tills, Jenny from the Wallington store has been selected to meet Her Royal Highness and she cannot wait. As the supermarket celebrates its past, it also looks to the future by trialling an experiment to remove all the checkouts from one store, encouraging customers to pay with their mobiles. Will it change the face of shopping or simply send customers elsewhere?(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Actor Danny Dyer wants to know more about his tough working-class family in the East End of London and hopes his history will 'freak a few people out'. But he gets more than he bargained for when he unearths an extraordinary lineage stretching back to the 11th century(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: Cash Trapped
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Bradley Walsh in which six contestants battle it out across a series of fast-paced question rounds where the players must actively try to sabotage their opponents by beating them to the buzzer and trapping them out of the game. programme.title: Stephen Fry in Central America
programme.desc: Stephen's travels continue in Costa Rica, where he encounters poison-dart frogs and falls in love with a baby sloth at a biological research station. But he soon finds there is more to the country than conservation and eco-tourism as he ascends volcanoes, descends white-water rivers and experiences the passion of a football derby in the capital of San Jose. He then crosses over into Panama for the final leg of his trip, witnessing giant leatherback turtles coming ashore to lay their eggs and learning how to dredge the canal in Panama City(n)

programme.title: Gino's Italian Escape
programme.desc: Chef Gino D'Acampo tours his homeland by train, taking in some of some of the most scenic train journeys in Europe. He begins in the French Alpine town of Breil-sur-Roya, so he can travel into Italy in style. Once in his own country, he meets a doctor who does his rounds on horseback, and visits hazelnut groves where he prepares a chocolate cheesecake. In Turin, he fulfils a lifelong dream of racing on top of the world-famous Lingotto rooftop track and makes his final dish - fettuccine pasta with mascarpone and pork mince(n)

programme.title: Brothers in Football
programme.desc: Following south London amateur football team Corinthian-Casuals as they travel to Sao Paulo in Brazil to fulfil a tour abandoned 100 years before on the eve of the First World War, facing two-time FIFA Club World Cup-winners SC Corinthians Paulista. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: Cash Trapped
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Bradley Walsh in which six contestants battle it out across a series of fast-paced question rounds where the players must actively try to sabotage their opponents by beating them to the buzzer and trapping them out of the game. In the final round, the person who has earned the most cash will go head to head against the rest of the group to try to take their winnings home(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Bernice is left conflicted over a request, Chas accepts help from a surprising source, and Dawn feels dejected(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale

programme.title: Gino's Italian Escape
programme.desc: Chef Gino D'Acampo tours his homeland by train, taking in some of some of the most scenic train journeys in Europe. He begins in the French Alpine town of Breil-sur-Roya, so he can travel into Italy in style. Once in his own country, he meets a doctor who does his rounds on horseback, and visits hazelnut groves where he prepares a chocolate cheesecake. In Turin, he fulfils a lifelong dream of racing on top of the world-famous Lingotto rooftop track and makes his final dish - fettuccine pasta with mascarpone and pork mince(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth falls victim to depression after failing to prevent the murder of a society girl by notorious art thief Marrascaud. His confidence shattered, Poirot eventually returns to work when a lonely chauffeur begs him to find his missing soulmate, the maid of a famous Russian dancer. The investigation takes him to the Swiss Alps, where, against all odds, he stumbles upon a hotel thought to be Marrascaud's hideout - leading to a very personal showdown. The penultimate episode of the long-running detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Simon Callow, Orla Brady, Patrick Ryecart, Nigel Lindsay, Morven Christie, Tom Austen and Rupert Evans(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot

He investigates the disappearance of a local cook and becomes intrigued by the contents of the woman's trunk. David Suchet stars, alongside Hugh Fraser and Brigit Forsyth(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The GP returns to his former post at the surgery after the departure of Dr Dibbs, agreeing to stay until a replacement is found. He persuades a reluctant Louisa to move into his house so he can help raise their son, and suggests they could all move to London together in the future. Martin then delivers the eulogy at his aunt Joan's funeral and speaks to her sister Ruth, who proves to be just as cantankerous as he is. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz, with Eileen Atkins(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the discovery of an unidentified teenage boy's body in a coastal town's shipyard, who is revealed to be listed as a missing person, having absconded from the care of his support worker. Vera discovers that the quiet community where he was found is plagued by a serious drugs problem - despite the fact that the only known dealer in the area has turned his back on crime. The victim could be the answer to the mystery, but a wall of silence surrounds his death. Mark Addy guest stars(n)

programme.title: Innocent
programme.desc: As DI Hudson's new investigation unearths serious flaws in the original murder investigation, those who were closest to Tara are forced to explain away credible motives for wanting to kill her. Relationships are strained to breaking and damaging accusations fly when David confronts his brother and sister in law over access to his children(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. The team tries to find out who was responsible for the brutal stabbing of a fellow officer, but their dogged police work is thwarted when it is proven that their main suspect is not to blame. Meanwhile, as Chief Superintendent Anderson puts pressure on Helen to close the case, Banks becomes certain that one man is the perpetrator, but when his obsession sends him over the edge, Anderson questions if he is up to the job. Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson, Andrea Lowe and Caroline Catz(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Conclusion. Officially on 'gardening leave', Banks is forced to operate on the other side of the law for the first time, as he uses his years of experience to secure an all-important breakthrough in his mission to bring crime boss Steve Richards to justice. However, an unexpected turn of events pushes the grizzled DCI and his team back to square one, and Banks comes to understand a one-on-one confrontation may be his only chance to bring down Richards. Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: David Suchet takes on his final case as the Belgian sleuth in the last-ever episode of the long-running drama. Crippled with arthritis, Poirot is now using a wheelchair and has a life-threatening heart condition, but remains as sharp as ever. Calling on old friend Captain Hastings for assistance, he returns to Styles, where the pair first met 30 years earlier on a murder investigation. The detective is convinced the country house is once again harbouring a killer, and as guests fall victim to a mysterious attacker, he must summon the last of his strength to battle his ultimate nemesis. With Hugh Fraser, Anne Reid, Philip Glenister, Helen Baxendale, Matthew McNulty, Anna Madeley and Shaun Dingwall(n)

programme.desc: A barmaid's murder leads Lewis (Kevin Whately) and Hathaway (Laurence Fox) to a respected professor, who admits he once had an affair with the woman. However, they discover the killing was copied straight from the pages of a popular fantasy novel written by an Oxford tutor, suggesting it may be the work of a crazed fan - or something even more sinister. Guest starring Art Malik, Tom Mison and James Fox, father of Laurence(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A new recruit reports for his first day of work at the police station, but given his family's history in Aidensfield, not all of the villagers are pleased to see him. Meanwhile, Ben and Liz's fund-raising efforts appear to have rubbed off on Vernon, who begins to show his charitable side(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: English country squire Marcus Waverly receives a series of anonymous letters threatening to kidnap his son Johnnie, but the case is dismissed by Scotland Yard - prompting Poirot to open his own investigation. Drama, starring David Suchet and Geoffrey Bateman(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa's hippie mother Eleanor (Louise Jameson) arrives in the village, immediately taking over Martin's kitchen to prepare a herbal tonic, which she later gives to the baby to stop his crying. A culinary rivalry develops between Bert and Mark Bridge, the landlord of the Crab and Lobster pub, at Portwenn Fun Day, with both keen to take advantage of the event by providing food for the spectators(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective reopens an unsolved case when a skeleton is discovered beneath the burned-out remains of a Whitley Bay nightclub. She suspects the bones belong to a notorious criminal who was once a friend of her father and has been missing for more than 20 years. When another murder occurs nearby, she investigates the possibility that a local crime family may be involved with both deaths, while the original owners of the club also have secrets to hide. Brenda Blethyn stars in an adaptation of Ann Cleeves' 2017 Vera Stanhope novel The Seagull(n)

programme.title: Innocent
programme.desc: David's exoneration allows him to regain custody of his children and intensifies the investigation into Rob, Alice and Tom, leading to a critical arrest. But as damaging accusations concerning DI Hudson's relationship with David threaten to derail the case, her determination to deliver justice finally begins to unravel the mystery of who killed Tara. Drama, starring Lee Ingleby, Hermione Norris, Daniel Ryan and Angel Coulby(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maud goes to see a finance company, Charlie calls but Bet cannot bring herself to forgive him, and Derek is furious with Mavis for letting the coach go without him(n)

programme.desc: A flu epidemic hits the depot, improving Stan's financial health as he and Jack clock up overtime. Vintage comedy, starring Reg Varney(n)

programme.desc: The new depot nurse moves in as the Butlers' lodger - causing problems for Arthur. Vintage comedy, starring Reg Varney and Michael Robbins(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Robin find a rare plant in the Ropers' garden. Comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective finds himself investigating the strange and gruesome murder of an elderly psychiatrist. Elsewhere, Poirot's old friend Ariadne Oliver is being pressed to uncover the truth behind two decade-old deaths. As the pair dig deep for evidence, they soon discover that their separate investigations are linked and join forces to solve the overarching mystery. David Suchet, Zoe Wanamaker and Greta Scacchi star(n)

programme.desc: Liz has a job interview as assistant manager at a wine bar, Derek feels humiliated over the robbery, and Bernard is adamant he is giving up the church because of his loss of faith(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mavis is upset by Derek's coldness towards her, Tanya is upset when her father dumps her stuff at the Rovers, Des asks Raquel out, and Mike insists that Mark must stay at Oakhill(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth falls victim to depression after failing to prevent the murder of a society girl by notorious art thief Marrascaud. His confidence shattered, Poirot eventually returns to work when a lonely chauffeur begs him to find his missing soulmate, the maid of a famous Russian dancer. The investigation takes him to the Swiss Alps, where, against all odds, he stumbles upon a hotel thought to be Marrascaud's hideout - leading to a very personal showdown. The penultimate episode of the long-running detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Simon Callow, Orla Brady, Patrick Ryecart, Nigel Lindsay, Morven Christie, Tom Austen and Rupert Evans(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper Belgian detective, who usually only accepts cases of national importance, bends his rules for once and finds himself in run-down Clapham. He investigates the disappearance of a local cook and becomes intrigued by the contents of the woman's trunk. David Suchet stars, alongside Hugh Fraser and Brigit Forsyth(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The GP returns to his former post at the surgery after the departure of Dr Dibbs, agreeing to stay until a replacement is found. He persuades a reluctant Louisa to move into his house so he can help raise their son, and suggests they could all move to London together in the future. Martin then delivers the eulogy at his aunt Joan's funeral and speaks to her sister Ruth, who proves to be just as cantankerous as he is. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz, with Eileen Atkins(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the discovery of an unidentified teenage boy's body in a coastal town's shipyard, who is revealed to be listed as a missing person, having absconded from the care of his support worker. Vera discovers that the quiet community where he was found is plagued by a serious drugs problem - despite the fact that the only known dealer in the area has turned his back on crime. The victim could be the answer to the mystery, but a wall of silence surrounds his death. Mark Addy guest stars(n)

programme.title: Innocent
programme.desc: As DI Hudson's new investigation unearths serious flaws in the original murder investigation, those who were closest to Tara are forced to explain away credible motives for wanting to kill her. Relationships are strained to breaking and damaging accusations fly when David confronts his brother and sister in law over access to his children(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. The team tries to find out who was responsible for the brutal stabbing of a fellow officer, but their dogged police work is thwarted when it is proven that their main suspect is not to blame. Meanwhile, as Chief Superintendent Anderson puts pressure on Helen to close the case, Banks becomes certain that one man is the perpetrator, but when his obsession sends him over the edge, Anderson questions if he is up to the job. Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson, Andrea Lowe and Caroline Catz(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Conclusion. Officially on 'gardening leave', Banks is forced to operate on the other side of the law for the first time, as he uses his years of experience to secure an all-important breakthrough in his mission to bring crime boss Steve Richards to justice. However, an unexpected turn of events pushes the grizzled DCI and his team back to square one, and Banks comes to understand a one-on-one confrontation may be his only chance to bring down Richards. Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: David Suchet takes on his final case as the Belgian sleuth in the last-ever episode of the long-running drama. Crippled with arthritis, Poirot is now using a wheelchair and has a life-threatening heart condition, but remains as sharp as ever. Calling on old friend Captain Hastings for assistance, he returns to Styles, where the pair first met 30 years earlier on a murder investigation. The detective is convinced the country house is once again harbouring a killer, and as guests fall victim to a mysterious attacker, he must summon the last of his strength to battle his ultimate nemesis. programme.desc: New receptionist Morwenna Newcross (Jessica Ransom) gets off to a bad start after arriving late on her first day, and her hyperactive behaviour leads Martin to believe she is high on drugs. programme.title: The Age of Stupid (2008)
programme.desc: Drama documentary examining the impact of climate change. In an environmentally devastated future, the last man alive looks back at footage from 2008 to uncover the reasons why the human race did so little to prevent climate change before it was too late, examining real life stories including a windfarm developer fighting public opposition and an elderly mountain guide who has witnessed the melting of Alpine glaciers first-hand(n)

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Primary School
programme.desc: This edition of the documentary about life at Gascoigne Primary School in Barking, east London, follows three very different boys the school supports in various ways. Year 5 pupil Cleyson is on the 'gifted and talented' list, but he has a hot temper and has recently been temporarily excluded for hurting another pupil, and staff are keen to help him avoid offending again as it could jeopardise his education. The school assigns teaching assistant Luke Brown to support 11-year-old Lewis, who has ADHD, while seven-year-old Toby is finding settling down in Year 2 a slow process(n)

programme.title: The Age of Stupid (2008)
programme.desc: Drama documentary examining the impact of climate change. In an environmentally devastated future, the last man alive looks back at footage from 2008 to uncover the reasons why the human race did so little to prevent climate change before it was too late, examining real life stories including a windfarm developer fighting public opposition and an elderly mountain guide who has witnessed the melting of Alpine glaciers first-hand(n)

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner(n)

programme.title: Tommy Cooper
programme.desc: The incomparable comedian performs a variety of sketches, guest starring Richard Briers and assistance from Janet Webb, Paul Bacon and Gordon Stothard(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar finds a suspicious building in Wales which is being used as a research laboratory, plunging him into the midst of a spine-chilling adventure. Spy drama, directed by and starring Roger Moore(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Theo works with the FBI on apprehending a drug dealer, an operation that leaves a police officer dead. The FBI agents prove less than helpful when it comes to catching the killer(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: Sam hands in his notice, but not before unsuccessfully trying to dissuade Quincy from investigating the sudden death of a Japanese martial arts movie star(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Apparently successful entrepreneur Clive Cosgrove loses his 'confidence' after Arthur and Terry attend his wedding celebrations. Vintage comedy drama, guest starring Patrick Mower, with George Cole and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A member of a group of convicts slips off the roof and dies during a robbery. The apparent leader of the gang appears to be having doubts about his life and Regan senses he is deliberately pointing clues in a desire to be caught during his next job. John Thaw and Dennis Waterman star(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: A captured British spy is released when his Russian counterpart falls into British hands. C15 is tasked with ensuring his safety as well as establishing whether his information is genuine. However, such intentions fail miserably when he is poisoned. Bodie and Doyle set out to determine what part his daughter, a concert pianist, played in his death - assuming she really is his daughter(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar investigates the suspicious deaths of five company directors, who each received a card with the words 'The Scales of Justice' printed on it prior to their demise. However, he finds his inquiries frustrated by the remaining members of the board. Roger Moore stars(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Theo poses as a Greek millionaire to investigate murder and robbery on Wall Street - but is kidnapped when his cover is blown. Directed by Richard Donner (Superman, Lethal Weapon)(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: A high-school football player ignores Quincy's warnings to give up the sport, even though there is a risk that the exertion might cause him to die of the same congenital condition that killed his elder brother. Drama, starring Jack Klugman(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry is assigned to mind a writer of romantic fiction with a drink problem, whose ex-husband has sold their house behind her back. Beryl Reid and Bill Maynard guest star, alongside George Cole and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: A ministerial secretary innocently leaks information regarding a secret peace talks meeting to her lover, leading to a shooting incident and placing the future of the summit in jeopardy. While her boyfriend's involvement remains in doubt, Cowley gets her to pass on false information in the hope it will prove his innocence or confirm his guilt. Drama, starring Gordon Jackson, Lewis Collins and Martin Shaw(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A bad dream has Regan's girlfriend fearing for his safety, and her visions ring true when he gets involved in a case involving a stolen lorryload of cigars. The plot thickens when illicit cocaine is retrieved and arms stolen from the US are discovered to be on their way to a military faction in Ireland. John Thaw and Dennis Waterman star(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Nicki, Hannah, Michelle and Mark pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Fishing Allstars
programme.desc: Dean Macey and Adam Rooney travel across Italy, with their main target a monster wels catfish of up to 300lbs in the River Po. They also aim to catch smaller channel catfish from the River Arno and carp from a stunning mountain lake near Bologna(n)

programme.title: Payback (1999)
programme.desc: A small-time crook is betrayed and left for dead by his wife and partner in crime. A year later, he returns seeking his share of their ill-gotten gains - but his former friend has gone up in the underworld and getting his due means taking on a powerful crime syndicate. Remake of 1967 thriller Point Blank, starring Mel Gibson, Kris Kristofferson, Gregg Henry, Maria Bello, James Coburn and Lucy Liu(n)

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest entertainment news(n)

programme.title: A Fistful of Dynamite (1971)
programme.desc: An IRA explosives expert fleeing the British authorities lies low in Mexico, just as the revolution erupts. He is persuaded to join forces with a bandit to rob a bank, but they end up becoming unwitting heroes of the revolutionary cause. Sergio Leone's Spaghetti Western, starring Rod Steiger, James Coburn, Maria Monti, Romolo Valli, Rik Battaglia and Franco Graziosi(n)

programme.title: Kojak

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: Sam hands in his notice, but not before unsuccessfully trying to dissuade Quincy from investigating the sudden death of a Japanese martial arts movie star(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Apparently successful entrepreneur Clive Cosgrove loses his 'confidence' after Arthur and Terry attend his wedding celebrations. Vintage comedy drama, guest starring Patrick Mower, with George Cole and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A member of a group of convicts slips off the roof and dies during a robbery. John Thaw and Dennis Waterman star(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: A captured British spy is released when his Russian counterpart falls into British hands. C15 is tasked with ensuring his safety as well as establishing whether his information is genuine. However, such intentions fail miserably when he is poisoned. Bodie and Doyle set out to determine what part his daughter, a concert pianist, played in his death - assuming she really is his daughter(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar investigates the suspicious deaths of five company directors, who each received a card with the words 'The Scales of Justice' printed on it prior to their demise. However, he finds his inquiries frustrated by the remaining members of the board. Roger Moore stars(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Theo poses as a Greek millionaire to investigate murder and robbery on Wall Street - but is kidnapped when his cover is blown. Directed by Richard Donner (Superman, Lethal Weapon)(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: A high-school football player ignores Quincy's warnings to give up the sport, even though there is a risk that the exertion might cause him to die of the same congenital condition that killed his elder brother. Drama, starring Jack Klugman(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry is assigned to mind a writer of romantic fiction with a drink problem, whose ex-husband has sold their house behind her back. Beryl Reid and Bill Maynard guest star, alongside George Cole and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: A ministerial secretary innocently leaks information regarding a secret peace talks meeting to her lover, leading to a shooting incident and placing the future of the summit in jeopardy. While her boyfriend's involvement remains in doubt, Cowley gets her to pass on false information in the hope it will prove his innocence or confirm his guilt. Drama, starring Gordon Jackson, Lewis Collins and Martin Shaw(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A bad dream has Regan's girlfriend fearing for his safety, and her visions ring true when he gets involved in a case involving a stolen lorryload of cigars. The plot thickens when illicit cocaine is retrieved and arms stolen from the US are discovered to be on their way to a military faction in Ireland. John Thaw and Dennis Waterman star(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Nicki, Hannah, Michelle and Mark pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. programme.title: Fishing Allstars

programme.title: Payback (1999)
programme.desc: A small-time crook is betrayed and left for dead by his wife and partner in crime. A year later, he returns seeking his share of their ill-gotten gains - but his former friend has gone up in the underworld and getting his due means taking on a powerful crime syndicate. Remake of 1967 thriller Point Blank, starring Mel Gibson, Kris Kristofferson, Gregg Henry, Maria Bello, James Coburn and Lucy Liu(n)

programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George Clarke helps unlock the potential of two houses in south London. He begins by helping NHS theatre nurse Hannah, whose Edwardian worker's cottage in Thornton Heath is so small she has had to give the only decent bedroom to her two children while she sleeps in the front room. Can he provide the luxurious master bedroom she needs? George also helps a couple renovate their Streatham home, which would not be so bad if not for the previous owners' love of glass chandeliers, Roman columns and marble-effect tiles(n)

programme.title: Boeing's Killer Plane: What Went Wrong?
programme.desc: Unravelling the events that led to two modern passenger jets falling out of the sky, and why the story of the fastest-selling aircraft in Boeing's history ended in tragedy. On 29 October 2018, Lion Air Flight 610 crashed into the Java Sea, and all 189 people on board died. Less than five months later, on 10 March 2019, an Ethiopian Airlines 737 MAX also crashed, and the 157 people on board were all killed. The documentary examines the crash reports to explore how these catastrophes could have been avoided, using state-of-the-art flight simulation and vivid CGI sequences to chart the flights' final moment(n)

programme.title: Shocking Emergency Calls UK
programme.desc: A chilling call from an online predator in Essex leads to a confession of brutal murder, and in north London, emergency services respond to a horrifying incident after a van deliberately driven into a crowd outside a mosque. Elsewhere, a harrowing call is taken in the aftermath of a frenzied acid attack by a woman on her partner in Bristol(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The return of the documentary about life inside an A&E department, following patients treated in the same 24-hour period at St George's in south-west London. Fourteen-year-old Eduard is rushed in after falling from a swing and fracturing his ankle, while 62-year-old Rosina is treated having broken her wrist on the dancefloor at a family party(n)

programme.title: Flirty Dancing
programme.desc: Although a big romcom fan, Emmerson has had his heart broken in the past and finds it hard to trust. He is matched with 28-year-old Ruby, whose dreams of becoming a professional ballet dancer were shattered when she had to have both hips replaced. Ashley Banjo creates a dance with a challenging drop that relies on trusting each other from the very first moment they meet. He also assists property manager Garry, who finds it hard to talk to men he fancies. Ashley's energetic, playful routine gives him and Ryan - who has never had a boyfriend before - the opportunity to put themselves 'out there'(n)

programme.title: The British Tribe Next Door
programme.desc: The final episode finds the Moffatt family and the Himba sharing one last week together. Mark shows his appreciation to the village for their hospitality by throwing a traditional British barbecue, while Scarlett and Betty discover that the intermittent drought - a regular feature of the environment - means the villagers are no longer able to grow enough crops, so the Himba women have to travel to the local town to shop for food. The final night rapidly approaches and a party is thrown to mark the occasion, followed by emotional goodbyes and the swapping of treasured keepsakes(n)

programme.title: 45 Years (2015)

The message exposes the cracks in their relationship. Drama, starring Charlotte Rampling, Tom Courtenay and Geraldine James(n)

programme.title: The Accident

programme.title: Alaska: A Year in the Wild
programme.desc: Winter's grip loosens as spring arrives in the northern American state. As the days lengthen and temperatures rise, a transformation is triggered that ranks as one of the most spectacular on the continent. However, spring in Alaska is short as the creatures have less than two months to feed, mate and raise a family - all the while avoiding the attentions of predators. The season also sees tens of millions of migrating birds return to the region, and hungry creatures re-emerge from hibernation, such as the grizzly bear(n)

programme.title: Great Artists
programme.desc: Historian Tim Marlow explores the life and work of 17th-century visionary Dutch artist Rembrandt Harmenszoon van Rijn, who was famed for his skilful handling of light and shade in paintings portraying the human form. Born in Leiden in 1606, the eighth of nine children, his passion for art led him to an apprenticeship with a local painter, and from the time he was a 19-year-old he had a studio in Amsterdam, where he produced 75 self-portraits. Demand for his talents soon grew and success came when he was commissioned to paint The Anatomy Lesson of Dr Tulp(n)
