programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: The Scottish National Party(n)

programme.title: Alaska: A Year in the Wild
programme.desc: Autumn draws in and animals prepare for the hard winter ahead. For many young creatures, this is the point where their mothers leave them to fend for themselves. Humpback whales return to feeding grounds on the south east coast, having completed one of the longest migrations of any species, while brown bears compete for the best spots to catch salmon before their winter hibernation. Also returning to Alaska are an astonishing 300 million salmon, all looking to spawn in the very same streams in which they hatched(n)

programme.title: How to Stop Your Nuisance Calls
programme.desc: An investigation into the plague of nuisance and scam callers, such as incessant calls about PPI or accidents that never happened. Alexis Conran tells the stories of victims like Jeff, who lost £20,000 to fraudsters pretending to be his bank, and Jessica, who was brainwashed into thinking she was being monitored by the FBI. Alexis also meets police, bank representatives and members of the call centre industry to ask whether enough is being done to help, and reveals just what people can do to stop these callers(n)

programme.title: Cold Call
programme.desc: With nowhere else to turn and tensions rising, June and Kirk are both pushed to breaking point. Drama about a fraud victim persuaded to go to extreme lengths to get to the bottom of the criminal enterprise that targeted her, starring Sally Lindsay and Paul Higgins(n)

programme.title: Good Girls' Guide to Kinky Sex
programme.desc: The bedroom experiment continues as three self-confessed good girls and their guys look for creative ways to improve their sex lives. They experiment with temperature play, discovering ways of turning their skin and senses wild with ice cubes and lubricants, before trying out a selection of sex toys. Finally they try a little role play by acting out their partner's ultimate fantasy for the night(n)

programme.title: Gypsy Kids: Our Secret World
programme.desc: The documentary follows 12-year-old English traveller Royston, who spends most of his spare time in the gym training. His first fight was with a bully at school and now he has several titles and is on course to turn professional. But he is not the only one. Twins Johnny and Abraham have also had trouble with bullies and have resorted to fighting back, so before they get into trouble at school, their uncle - a professional cage fighter - is taking them under his wing in a training session. Meanwhile, 10-year-old Annie recently moved from the site where she was born into a house with her mother, but she is missing her friends(n)

programme.title: The Odessa File (1974)
programme.desc: The detailed diaries of a former inmate of a concentration camp fall into the hands of an investigative journalist. Incensed by what he reads, he embarks on a crusade to discover the prison commandant's new identity and expose his crimes to the world. In the process, the trail leads him to a secret organisation that helped Nazi SS officers escape justice. Thriller, based on Frederick Forsyth's novel, starring Jon Voight, Derek Jacobi and siblings Maria and Maximilian Schell(n)

programme.title: Support Your Local Sheriff! (1969)
programme.desc: A drifter in need of money takes the job of sheriff in a small mining outpost, and chooses the town drunk to be his deputy. Events take a chaotic turn when a family of influential crooks decides to pull out all the stops to do away with the lawman. Comedy Western, starring James Garner, Joan Hackett, Jack Elam, Bruce Dern and Walter Brennan(n)

programme.title: Invitation to a Gunfighter (1964)
programme.desc: A Civil War veteran returns home to find a ruthless landowner has sold the family house and his land has been confiscated as so-called enemy property. When the soldier retaliates by breaking into his former home and barricading himself in, the land baron hires a mysterious gunslinger to force the rebel out - but once unleashed, the hired gun has ideas of his own. Western, starring Yul Brynner, George Segal, Janice Rule and Pat Hingle(n)

programme.title: The Guns of Fort Petticoat (1957)
programme.desc: A lieutenant deserts after his commanding officer launches an unprovoked attack on a Comanche village during the Civil War. Realising that there are no soldiers in his home town to protect it from reprisals, he returns to train the local women to defend themselves. Western, starring Audie Murphy, Kathryn Grant, Hope Emerson and Jeff Donnell(n)

programme.title: The Way Way Back (2013)
programme.desc: An introverted teenager joins his mother and her overbearing boyfriend on an awkward family holiday. He befriends a free-spirited employee at the nearby water park, and the relationship inspires him to assert his independence for the first time. Comedy drama, starring Liam James, Sam Rockwell, Steve Carell, Toni Collette and Allison Janney(n)

programme.title: The Great Wall (2016)
programme.desc: In the 11th century, a group of Europeans set out for China to discover how to make gunpowder. However, on arrival their claims to be on a trading mission are disbelieved and they are arrested and imprisoned by the Nameless Order within the Great Wall - just as it is about to be beset by giant, lizard-like monsters. As wave after wave of attacks and surprise tactics batter the Wall, Europeans and Chinese battle hoards of the alien creatures in a series of escalating conflicts leading to a final confrontation with the alien queen. Fantasy adventure, starring Matt Damon, Willem Dafoe and Tian Jing(n)

programme.title: Conan the Barbarian (2011)
programme.desc: An evil warlord kills a barbarian chieftain, whose son grows up to be a mighty warrior and seeks revenge for his father's death. He finds there is more at stake, as the tyrant is close to acquiring all the pieces of a magic mask, which will make him all-powerful. Fantasy adventure, with Jason Momoa, Stephen Lang, Ron Perlman and Rose McGowan(n)

programme.title: The Mortal Instruments: City of Bones (2013)
programme.desc: A teenager discovers she is descended from a race of half-human, half-angel warriors whose duty is to defend the world from demons. When her mother disappears, she joins a group of the magical beings on a dangerous journey into an alternate version of New York inhabited by strange creatures. Fantasy adventure, starring Lily Collins, Jamie Campbell Bower, Lena Headey and Jonathan Rhys Meyers(n)

programme.title: The Last Command (1955)
programme.desc: Adventurer Jim Bowie returns to Texas during the 1830s and tries to quell the simmering antagonism between settlers and the Mexican government. However, as the Mexican army's aggression intensifies, he is forced to take up arms and fight in the historic battle at the Alamo. Western, starring Sterling Hayden, Arthur Hunnicutt, Anna Maria Alberghetti and Richard Carlson(n)

programme.title: The Great Sioux Massacre (1965)
programme.desc: A corrupt senator coaxes Colonel Custer out of retirement with the prospect of presidential office, and the soldier tries to further his political ambitions by wresting control of the current military campaign from its commanding officer, culminating in his last stand at the Battle of the Little Bighorn. Western, with Philip Carey, Joseph Cotten, Darren McGavin and Julie Sommars(n)

programme.title: In Harm's Way (1965)
programme.desc: Two Navy officers are disgraced in the aftermath of the attack on Pearl Harbour, one for a disastrous engagement with the enemy and the other for a drunken brawl after learning of his wife's death. They get another chance to prove their worth when they are placed in charge of a vital counter-offensive. Second World War drama, starring John Wayne, Kirk Douglas and Henry Fonda(n)

programme.title: Back to the Future (1985)
programme.desc: Teenager Marty McFly travels back to the 1950s in a time machine built by his eccentric inventor friend. The return journey proves tricky enough, but he also accidentally alters the sequence of events that led to his parents falling in love - and has to persuade his future dad to win his mother's heart, or he will never be born. Sci-fi comedy adventure, starring Michael J Fox, Christopher Lloyd, Lea Thompson and Crispin Glover(n)

programme.title: Gods of Egypt (2016)
programme.desc: An ancient Egyptian god is driven into exile when his evil brother murders their father and seizes his throne. He enlists the aid of a human thief in restoring justice, and in return offers to help the mortal to bring his murdered lover back to life. Fantasy adventure, starring Nikolaj Coster-Waldau, Brenton Thwaites, Gerard Butler and Chadwick Boseman(n)

programme.title: Dawn of the Planet of the Apes (2014)
programme.desc: A colony of super-intelligent apes thrives following a global virus outbreak that wipes out much of the human race. A survivor tries to make peace with the leader of the apes, but their efforts to reach an understanding are threatened by warmongers on both sides. Sci-fi thriller sequel, starring Andy Serkis, Jason Clarke and Gary Oldman(n)

programme.title: Powder Room (2013)
programme.desc: A woman recovering from a series of setbacks is reunited with an old friend from college, who is now a successful businesswoman living in Paris. During an evening at a nightclub, she begins spinning a web of lies to make her life seem more impressive - but her efforts are ruined by the arrival of her rowdy mates. Comedy, starring Sheridan Smith, Kate Nash, Jaime Winstone and Oona Chaplin(n)

programme.title: Dark Command (1940)

Before long he has a reputation as a feared raider fighting on the side of the South, and it falls to the newly appointed lawman whose job he coveted to stop his reign of terror. Cold War thriller and the second film to feature Len Deighton's character, starring Michael Caine, Eva Renzi, Oscar Homolka, Paul Hubschmid and Guy Doleman(n)

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: Architect George Clarke and master craftsman Will Hardie embark on a road trip to explore the ingenious small-space wonders hidden away among the snowy peaks of the Swiss, French and Italian Alps. They start out from an observation point 4,000 metres above sea level, then take in a secret ski lodge disguised as a boulder, a treehouse resembling a giant acorn and a Swiss cabin made almost entirely from concrete. Other highlights include a hamlet made from eight 200-year-old chalets collected from all over the Alps and a mountaintop restaurant resembling a spaceship(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 71-year-old woman is rushed in after being knocked over by her husband's car, and her blood pressure has dropped critically low. Four-year-old Maddi is brought in with an injured neck after falling over on a train, and doctors worry that metal plates she already had in her neck may have shifted, causing damage to her spine. Plus, 68-year-old Bonnie arrives after hitting her head on her bedside table(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the words and numbers quiz, with Vic Reeves and Sara Pascoe joining team captains Sean Lock and Lee Mack. Alex Horne and the Horne Section join Countdown regular Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley provides the numbers and letters(n)

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: A tennis player is put into an induced coma after suffering a heart attack on the court, a fell runner is seriously injured by cows, and a man is crushed under a collapsed wall(n)

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: Architect George Clarke and master craftsman Will Hardie embark on a road trip to explore the ingenious small-space wonders hidden away among the snowy peaks of the Swiss, French and Italian Alps. They start out from an observation point 4,000 metres above sea level, then take in a secret ski lodge disguised as a boulder, a treehouse resembling a giant acorn and a Swiss cabin made almost entirely from concrete. Other highlights include a hamlet made from eight 200-year-old chalets collected from all over the Alps and a mountaintop restaurant resembling a spaceship(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The fiery chef tries to change the fortunes of Finn McCool's, a traditional Irish pub in the Hamptons, New York. The chances of the establishment becoming a success are being ruined by the owner Buddy Mazzio's two sons, bartender Jason and chef Brian, who are constantly fighting with each other, prompting Gordon to have a heart-to-heart with the family(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty heads on the trail of an elusive fruit - the fresh date, visiting a vast water purification plant in Israel where the raw sewage of Tel Aviv is harnessed to turn barren desert into fertile farmland. Kate Quilton flies to California to investigate why almond butter is more expensive than other nutty spreads, where she discovers a combination of extreme weather and global economics have affected the price of almonds. Matt Tebbutt makes a surprising discovery about the true origins of haggis(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: The team members select some of their favourite investigations from the show. Putting plums, prunes and prune juice to the test, Jimmy Doherty heads to King's College London to make a concoction that will help keep people's bowel movements regular. Kate Quilton visits Vietnam to learn about the difference between black and white pepper, and discovers the remarkable lengths to which producers go to guard their valuable crop from thieves, while Matt Tebbutt is in Switzerland to find out why there are holes in Swiss cheese(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Graham and Dawn Tucker love the beautiful Italian region of Le Marche and want to buy a perfect home from home there. With a budget of £160,000, Sara Damergi searches every nook and cranny of the local property market to help the couple find their ideal retreat(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Long-time friends Jackie Venn and Jane Howes adore Italy and have pooled together all their savings seeking a renovation project in the region of Abruzzo. They want a substantial property offering views, olive trees and a layout that suits their different needs, so turn to property expert Sara Damergi to help them achieve their objectives(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Robert Fox and his partner Kevin, who run Cherry Tree House in Blackpool, hope to be in with a chance of winning the title of best B&B as another round of the competition gets under way. However, two guests are shocked at the room prices, and an afternoon learning clown skills sees a North-South divide develop in the group(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Andy and Caroline Middleton host at the Old Hall Inn in North Yorkshire, where a couple of guests are unimpressed by a discovery in their bathroom, and the afternoon's angling activity leaves two more frustrated when they fail to catch a fish(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit is to George and Juliet Bristow's Pilot Boat Inn on the Isle of Wight, where some of the guests are amazed by the building - yet others are shocked to find cobwebs in their bedrooms. Also, the hosts' constant hovering at breakfast makes the diners feel uncomfortable(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Lucy Roe welcomes her guests to Rutland Terrace in Stamford, Lincolnshire, and although they are delighted with the elegant decor, George and Juliet are unimpressed by their lack of en-suite facilities. Kevin and Robert are horrified by the state of their mattress, before being accused of attention seeking at dinner(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances, before the winner is announced(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Theatre-lover Claire Flatt hosts the first party in Ipswich, where one guest makes a big impression by arriving in a limousine. Claire prepares Thai fishcakes and Princess Jasmine rice, as well as her signature dish 'chaos cake', in the hope of winning the £1,000 prize at the end of the week(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Millwall fan Kerrie Pankhurst hosts the second dinner party in Ipswich, which she decides to give a football theme - but the mood turns sour when one of the guests insults her team. Kerrie's decision to serve scallops and chorizo as her starter also does not go down well with DJ Dave James(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Magician David J Marks hosts the third dinner party in Ipswich, and hopes to win over Kerrie Pankhurst after offending her the previous night. His salmon starter impresses all the diners except Dave James, who explains all the gruesome things he would rather do than eat the fish, so the host lightens the mood by showing off his ventriloquist dummies and laying on a magic show(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: DJ Dave James hosts the fourth dinner party from Ipswich and asks his guests to attend dressed as pop stars. Claire Flatt and Nicki Yucel are suspicious about his celebrity connections, and there is trouble when the diners discover Dave obtained help with the cooking(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Speed-dating expert Nicki Yucel hosts the final dinner party in Ipswich, and is determined to cultivate a romantic atmosphere. As the evening progresses, Kerrie Pankhurst reveals her feelings for another diner, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: African painted dogs K'mana and Ville were thrown together as a breeding couple after the dominant female died, but K'mana was never Ville's first choice as partner and still carries a limp from her battles with Ville's previous mate. Now she is pregnant with the zoo's first ever litter of this rare and endangered species. Meanwhile, keepers try to rekindle interest between Okapi pair Dicky and Stuma, and Atlas moths Dave and Simon only have five days of life after hatching, and their sole purpose is to mate(n)

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: A mountain biker has a bad fall in a remote part of a forest, a four-year-old needs strong pain relief when he breaks his thigh bone, and a farmer fractures a replacement hip(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Frontline
programme.desc: A newly qualified paramedic treats a man who has suffered three epileptic seizures, and an air ambulance crew comes to the aid of an elderly man(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: As Dick and Angel prepare for winter, they attend a food festival in Rouen, where they hope to find inspiration over what to do with their fresh fruit and vegetables. Angel adds the finishing touches to the floating geodesic dome in time for a family sleepover, while Dick gets to work fixing the crumbling stucco(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A road traffic accident caused 52-year-old Mark to suffer cardiac arrest, and as he battles for survival at St George's Hospital, his wife Jacquie reminisces about how their relationship blossomed from friendship into romance. A severe hip dislocation seems to be the diagnosis when 25-year-old Kayley arrives in A&E after being thrown from her horse, and 23-year-old receptionist Melissa books an appointment with nurse practitioner Craig to find out why she is having trouble hearing(n)

programme.title: New Amsterdam
programme.desc: Max convinces Dr Sharpe to take a risk on his course of treatment, Bloom grapples with a difficult conversation and Frome works to help a transgender patient. Dr Kapoor considers helping his son in a big way after advice from a friend(n)

programme.title: American Pickers

programme.title: Storage Hunters

programme.title: Storage Hunters

programme.title: American Pickers

programme.title: Last Stop Garage

programme.title: Last Stop Garage
programme.desc: The boys build a rink-making machine to host a charity hockey game, and attach two snow-blowers to the front of an ATV in order to clear a mountain of snow off the local lake(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and James May take a look at increasingly popular tall hatchbacks known as crossovers. Having concluded their primary appeal is to caravanners, the duo spend a couple of days in models by Mazda and Volkswagen and try to get a feel for what life is like for the people they believe would buy one. Meanwhile, Richard Hammond drives a pair of exciting Lamborghinis in Italy - the ferocious Aventador Roadster and the limited-edition Sesto Elemento, which carries an eye-watering price tag of around £2million. Two cars are also put through their paces on the test track, with James in a modern re-creation of the classic Porsche 911 and Aerosmith frontman Steven Tyler in the rather less exotic Reasonably Priced Car(n)

programme.title: Last Stop Garage
programme.desc: When the only trail groomer in town breaks down on the 'snowmobile highway', the boys are called out to do some trail-side repairs(n)

programme.title: World's Most Dangerous Roads
programme.desc: Celebrities undertake some of the most perilous journeys in the world. Driving up Madagascar's eastern coast at the tail end of the rainy season is enough to test the best of friendships, but comedy actor Angus Deayton and broadcaster Mariella Frostrup are game enough to take on the challenge, and good enough friends to poke fun at each other along the way. The route has numerous river crossings and the duo face a variety of broken bridges and flimsy ferries as the only way across, while the roads themselves are mired with thick mud. Progress becomes increasingly painful and they are finally forced to spend a night in the car(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson tests the new BMW M4 at the track, before switching to the German manufacturer's hybrid-powered i8 for a trip up the A1 to Whitby, North Yorkshire, where he decides which of the two he would rather take for the drive home. With Land Rover production ceasing in the UK later this year, Richard Hammond pays tribute to the 4x4 by attempting to drive one straight up the side of a dam, while James May puts the Mercedes AMG GT through its paces. Taking to the test track in the Reasonably Priced Car are Will Smith and Margot Robbie(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond is dropped into the remote, frozen wastes of Canada to test a watch with a built-in emergency beacon. The distress signal is sent directly to Jeremy Clarkson and James May, who get their hands on two machines and set off on an epic adventure to find their colleague. Plus, Jeremy puts the new 471bhp Lexus RC F coupe thought its paces, while The Fall and The X-Files star Gillian Anderson takes to the track in the Reasonably Priced Car(n)

programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May attempt to improve the ambulance, with the presenters all going their own separate ways to enhance one area they think could do with an overhaul. programme.desc: Comedians Phill Jupitus and Marcus Brigstocke head to Bolivia for a high-altitude trip, first driving along the infamous 'Road of Death', only to find themselves deep in bandit country. Disorientation takes them by surprise as they have to navigate Salar de Uyuni - a salt lake so vast that Buzz Aldrin could see it from space. Their goal is the ancient mountain city of Potosi, whose silver mines financed the Spanish empire in the 16th century and which is almost 5,000 metres above sea level(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The trio of motoring enthusiasts embark on another epic road trip, this time across western America in front-engined supercars. Jeremy Clarkson pilots the Lexus LFA, which has a spectacularly high-revving V10 powerplant, Richard Hammond gets behind the wheel of the muscular Dodge Viper, and James May opts for the latest incarnation of Aston Martin's Vanquish. On their journey from Las Vegas to Palm Springs via Los Angeles, the presenters tackle racing circuits and find themselves in a race against the police, before rushing to the Mexican border - and there's a terrifying penalty for the last man to arrive. Meanwhile, Fleetwood Mac drummer Mick Fleetwood rolls up his sleeves to thrash the Reasonably Priced Car(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and Richard Hammond try to solve the problems that increasingly complex cars pose to an ageing population. They design a vehicle for elderly people and put it to the test in Dorset with the help of three pensioners. Meanwhile, James May gets acquainted with a revamped Range Rover in London before pitting the luxurious off-roader against an autonomous military machine in Nevada. programme.title: Insert Name Here
programme.desc: Actor Stephen Mangan, TV presenter Mel Giedroyc, comedian Nish Kumar and historian Suzannah Lipscomb join host Sue Perkins and captains Richard Osman and Josh Widdicombe on the comedy panel show. In this edition, the two sides answer questions about famous people past and present who share the name Mary, with the winners earning the right to pick the greatest bearer of the moniker - prompting comic banter, funny facts and unusual stats as the teams vie to outdo each other(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: An evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, London. Jack Whitehall is the man in charge, treating the audience to his own brand of humour, before introducing routines by Canadian comedienne Katherine Ryan and American jokester Rich Hall(n)

programme.title: Jon Richardson: Ultimate Worrier
programme.desc: Jon and guests Richard Osman, Jessie Cave and Darren Harriott discuss, categorise and rank worries to do with Modern Living. Has inventing got out of hand? Is magic dead?(n)

programme.title: Comedians Giving Lectures
programme.desc: Stand-up comedians Ed Gamble, Katherine Ryan and Sindhu Vee are given the titles of real lectures and invited to give their own version. Presented by Sara Pascoe(n)

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Frank Skinner chairs the comedy panel show, inviting comedians Lee Mack and Ruby Wax and Hairy Biker Dave Myers to compete to have their pet hates banished for ever. Their gripes include Top Gear, new-age books and chunky chips(n)

programme.title: Slow Train Through Africa with Griff Rhys Jones
programme.desc: The comedian attempts to zigzag his way 1,200 miles through Kenya and Tanzania, beginning this leg of his journey on a vintage steam locomotive designed in Glasgow. On a cattle farm established by an aristocrat a century ago, he dons a pair of overalls and joins workers as they bathe more than 2,000 animals to ward off parasites and biting flies. Later, in the vast Tsavo East National Park, he bottle-feeds orphaned elephant calves, before visiting a Maasai village - where he quickly regrets a faux pas during a meat feast(n)

programme.title: Medieval Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: The suspicious death of Elizabethan noblewoman Amy Dudley, the wife of Robert Dudley, the favourite and alleged lover of Queen Elizabeth I and the man the monarch is said to have wanted to marry - if only he had been available. Amy's body was found at the foot of the stairs at Cumnor Place, her home in Oxfordshire in 1560, and her death caused a scandal - with the finger of blame pointed firmly at Dudley(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: The team visits Belton House stately home near Grantham, Lincolnshire, to investigate the creation of the Machine Gun Corps during the First World War. The regiment was set up in the grounds, but today there is virtually no sign of its existence and most regimental records have been destroyed. The dig, however, is rich in finds, revealing glimpses of the lives of the thousands of men who trained at the site, many of whom died in battle(n)

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: Examining the complex system the Nazis set up to implement the extermination of the Jewish race throughout Europe from 1942, with Auschwitz as the hub. The programme reveals that at other camps, including Treblinka, Belzec and Sobibor, rival commanders participated in the genocide with enthusiasm and shared ideas for the best method of mass murder(n)

programme.title: WW2 - Battles for Europe
programme.desc: The Allies reached the last great natural barrier between their forces and the German heartland. If they could get across the River Rhine, the war would be won(n)

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A fully relocatable polar research station in Antarctica, which was inspired by a revolutionary icebreaking ship and ingenious designs for pre-fabricated buildings(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A settlement in Argentina destroyed by floods, a town in the US that has been erased, and a Soviet dream-town that turned into a nuclear nightmare(n)

programme.desc: The comedian begins the next leg of his trip in Kasama, northern Zambia. His first stop is the town of Mpika, where he meets college students training to be locomotive drivers. Next, in the capital Lusaka, he reveals how the first railways were built to access the country's rich mineral wealth, and watches craftsmen cutting emeralds and rubies - before being let loose on a pair of bejewelled cufflinks. Griff then reaches the spectacular Victoria Falls on his way to Zimbabwe's second-largest city, Bulawayo(n)

programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the team investigate an Iron Age hill fort in Cardiff that dominates a city housing estate, and explore whether the site could be the long-lost ancient capital of south Wales. programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: A look at how corruption became rife within the SS, with the money stolen from Jews finding its way into the pockets of the camp guards rather than funding the war effort. Many of the SS also enjoyed illicit sexual relationships with female prisoners. programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: The historians reveal how the railways transformed the British diet, rescuing a nation that was struggling to feed itself. Alex Langlands and Peter Ginn discover how the mass transportation of livestock gave birth to the traditional British roast, and Ruth Goodman is in Whitby, which became a thriving fishing town thanks to the North Yorkshire Moors Railway supplying its kippers. Plus, Hampshire's watercress line, which brought nutritional salad to the masses, and how the use of steam-powered engines revolutionised production at Britain's oldest brewery(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and his team head to an army training ground on Salisbury Plain to discover what lies beneath the Barrow Clump, a huge mound of earth built by Bronze Age people to bury their dead. More than 2,500 years later, the Anglo-Saxons re-used the location as a burial site, leaving behind some fascinating artefacts. The team is joined for the dig by a group of wounded soldiers whose members are being trained in archaeology as part of their rehabilitation(n)

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: The events of 1944, when the death toll rose substantially. programme.desc: Unravelling the events that led to two modern passenger jets falling out of the sky, and why the story of the fastest-selling aircraft in Boeing's history ended in tragedy. On 29 October 2018, Lion Air Flight 610 crashed into the Java Sea, and all 189 people on board died. Less than five months later, on 10 March 2019, an Ethiopian Airlines 737 MAX also crashed, and the 157 people on board were all killed. The documentary examines the crash reports to explore how these catastrophes could have been avoided, using state-of-the-art flight simulation and vivid CGI sequences to chart the flights' final moment(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Benson and Stabler are called to investigate three violent sexual attacks against women. They enlist the aid of Dr Rebecca Hendrix, a policewoman-turned-psychiatrist, who helps them track down a suspect - but he turns out to be homosexual. Mariska Hargitay stars(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: During a surveillance operation, Fin stumbles on a robbery in a supermarket and almost loses his life. The incident results in him receiving unwelcome attention from the media, but also provides a chance to atone for past mistakes(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The team investigates a nine-year-old's claims she has been molested by her football coach - and the inquiry prompts a string of other children to come forward with similar stories. Stabler struggles to cope with the case since it involves victims who are of a similar age to his daughters - and is taken aback by the child's subsequent confession that she lied about the identity of her abuser(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: DiNozzo teams up with his girlfriend, ATF agent Zoe Keates, to investigate the circumstances surrounding the death of a US Marine in what initially seems like a simple road accident. The team reveals the dead man did indeed die due to a broken neck sustained during a crash, but someone shot at him first. As DiNozzo and Keates pursue their inquiry, they discover the victim's death is connected to a controversial ATF plan to place traceable firearms in the criminal system, and then follow them to the heads of crime organisations. Naval crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly(n)

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: Teenager Kim Walderman dies in an explosion while shopping in a high-street boutique. Horatio and the team try to trace the source of the blast, and are shocked to discover that the dress the girl was trying on had been fitted with an explosive device. David Caruso, Emily Procter and Adam Rodriguez star(n)

programme.title: CSI: Miami

programme.title: Hawaii Five-0
programme.desc: Danno's search for the members of a terrorist cell leads to a terrifying life-or-death situation when he activates a suicide bomb rigged to explode at the slightest movement or touch. As technicians set to work to free the nervous cop, McGarrett tries to distract his friend and colleague by getting him to talk about the toughest case of his career, flashing back to Danno's days as a detective in New Jersey, when he and his partner Grace were held hostage by mobsters. Scott Caan and Alex O'Loughlin star, with Terrence Howard (Iron Man)(n)

programme.title: Elementary
programme.desc: Holmes is asked to investigate a bombing in New York, which caused the deaths of two people. Sifting through the evidence, he unearths a crime that has lain dormant for years and his search for the truth leads him to the wily CEO of a PR firm whom he suspects has something to hide. Meanwhile, Watson insists Holmes select a sponsor to support his sobriety at the rehab group. Modern-day Sherlock Holmes adventure, guest starring Lisa Edelstein (House) and Ato Essandoh (Blood Diamond), with Jonny Lee Miller and Lucy Liu(n)

programme.title: Paramedics

programme.title: Air Ambulance ER
programme.desc: Fly-on-the-wall documentary following the work of Britain's emergency helicopter medical services. It's all hands on deck to save the life of an 11-year-old boy who has been knocked down by a car on the Isle of Wight, resulting in a serious head injury. A tough granny proves remarkably calm about her extreme ankle break, a bit of laughing gas helps take away the pain for a paintballer injured at an activity centre and the clock is ticking when a brewing thunderstorm threatens a callout to a motorcyclist trapped in a ditch(n)

programme.title: Hawaii Five-0
programme.desc: A computer hacker is released from prison after serving six months for crossing the wrong people, only to walk straight into some masked thugs who bundle him into their car. Meanwhile, Mary arrives in Hawaii and discovers her mother is still alive, so McGarrett stages a reunion - but he doesn't bank on his sister bringing along the motor-mouthed OAP she's been caring for. programme.title: Britain's Biggest Primary School
programme.desc: This edition of the documentary about life at Gascoigne Primary School in Barking, east London, follows three very different boys the school supports in various ways. Year 5 pupil Cleyson is on the 'gifted and talented' list, but he has a hot temper and has recently been temporarily excluded for hurting another pupil, and staff are keen to help him avoid offending again as it could jeopardise his education. The school assigns teaching assistant Luke Brown to support 11-year-old Lewis, who has ADHD, while seven-year-old Toby is finding settling down in Year 2 a slow process(n)

programme.title: Doodlebug Summer
programme.desc: Hitler's V1 flying bombs were responsible for the deaths of nearly 6,000 people in London and the South of England. This episode features accounts from the men and women whose job was to fight the V1s from the ground and a report on the impact of a bomb that hit a chapel one Sunday morning, killing 121 people(n)

programme.title: The Age of Stupid (2008)
programme.desc: Drama documentary examining the impact of climate change. In an environmentally devastated future, the last man alive looks back at footage from 2008 to uncover the reasons why the human race did so little to prevent climate change before it was too late, examining real life stories including a windfarm developer fighting public opposition and an elderly mountain guide who has witnessed the melting of Alpine glaciers first-hand(n)

programme.title: Spooks

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: The agents observe jailed Algerian terrorist Malik, whom they suspect of planning an attack from his cell.
